text.title: MCP Server Implementation — TypeScript and Python — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the mcp-server-implementation methodology. Sources: Anthropic — Model Context Protocol specification 2025; GitHub: modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk; GitHub: modelcontextprotocol/python-sdk.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `code` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r1).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `code` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r2).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `code` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r3).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `code` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r4).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `code` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r5).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.
